name: What if my work comp claim for fume exposure gets denied?

mainEntity.name: What if my work comp claim for fume exposure gets denied?

acceptedAnswer.text: Appeal fast with better medical evidence; get a lawyer.
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Appeal within the strict deadline, usually 20-60 days.**

Common denials: lack of work link, pre-existing issues[2]. Gather more evidence like specialist reports[8]. Utah/Idaho have hearings/referees[1].

- Request denial reasons in writing.
- Submit new medical proof.
- Consider OSHA complaint.

Hire a lawyer experienced in appeals; many win on reconsideration. Don't miss deadlines[2].

description: Appeal fast with better medical evidence; get a lawyer.
